prompt: This method leaks database connections and will eventually exhaust the connection pool. What is the bug?
choices: rs.getInt(1) should be rs.getInt("COUNT(*)") to read by column name
PreparedStatement and ResultSet are never closed — wrap them in try-with-resources to guarantee cleanup
The Connection parameter should be created inside the method, not passed in
return must come after rs.close() or the ResultSet cannot be garbage collected
explanation: PreparedStatement and ResultSet hold database cursors and memory. If an exception is thrown, or even in normal flow here, they are never closed — leaking resources that accumulate until the connection pool is exhausted. Fix: try (PreparedStatement ps = conn.prepareStatement(...); ResultSet rs = ps.executeQuery()) { rs.next(); return rs.getInt(1); }
